title: Landolphia rufescens
intro: Landolphia rufescens, also known as African yam, is a climbing plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Landolphia rufescens include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, sore throat, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Landolphia rufescens is a species of plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. It is a type of evergreen shrub native to tropical Africa. This species is also known as the African bitter apple.
